title: marsdenia littoralis
intro: Marsdenia littoralis, also known as the coastal vine or sea-maiden's hair, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Marsdenia littoralis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and hypertension, as well as its use as a treatment for various gastrointestinal issues. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
